2026-04-18T18:09:04.2898260Z ##[group]Run # Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)
2026-04-18T18:09:04.2898768Z [36;1m# Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)[0m
2026-04-18T18:09:04.2899164Z [36;1m# Mask immediately to prevent timing vulnerabilities[0m
2026-04-18T18:09:04.2899499Z [36;1mAPI_KEY=$(openssl rand -base64 45 | tr -d '/+=')[0m
2026-04-18T18:09:04.2899785Z [36;1mecho "::add-mask::${API_KEY}"[0m
2026-04-18T18:09:04.2900007Z [36;1m[0m
2026-04-18T18:09:04.2900156Z [36;1mPORT=3001[0m
2026-04-18T18:09:04.2900334Z [36;1m[0m

2026-04-18T18:09:04.2900742Z [36;1m{[0m

2026-04-18T18:09:04.2901192Z [36;1m  echo "safe_outputs_port=${PORT}"[0m
2026-04-18T18:09:04.2901428Z [36;1m} >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"[0m

This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 901 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/901). If omitted, closes the issue that triggered this workflow (requires an issue event trigger).","type":["number","string"]},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"},"state_reason":{"description":"The reason for closing the issue. Use 'COMPLETED' for resolved issues, 'NOT_PLANNED' for issues that won't be addressed, or 'DUPLICATE' for duplicate issues. Defaults to 'COMPLETED'.","enum":["COMPLETED","NOT_PLANNED","DUPLICATE"],"type":"string"}},"required":["body"],"type":"object"},"name":"close_issue"},{"description":"Add a comment to an existing GitHub issue, pull request, or discussion. Use this to provide feedback, answer questions, or add information to an existing conversation. For creating new items, use create_issue, create_discussion, or create_pull_request instead. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 2 comment(s) can be added. Target: *. Supports reply_to_id for discussion threading.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"body":{"description":"The comment text in Markdown format. This is the 'body' field - do not use 'comment_body' or other variations. Provide helpful, relevant information that adds value to the conversation.","type":"string"},"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"item_number":{"description":"The issue, pull request, or discussion number to comment on. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 123 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/123). Can also be a temporary_id (e.g., 'aw_abc123') from a previously created issue in the same workflow run. If omitted, the tool will attempt to resolve the target from the current workflow context (triggering issue, PR, or discussion). If auto-targeting fails (e.g., for schedule or workflow_dispatch triggers), the tool call will fail with an error.","type":["number","string"]},"reply_to_id":{"description":"Node ID of the discussion comment to reply to, enabling threaded discussion comments. When provided, the new comment is posted as a reply to the specified top-level discussion comment. If the given node ID belongs to a nested reply, the handler automatically resolves it to the top-level parent. Only applicable for discussion comments — ignored for issue and pull request comments.","type":"string"},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"},"temporary_id":{"description":"Unique temporary identifier for this comment. Format: 'aw_' followed by 3 to 12 alphanumeric characters (e.g., 'aw_abc1', 'aw_Test123'). Auto-generated if not provided. The temporary ID is returned in the tool response so you can reference this comment later.","pattern":"^aw_[A-Za-z0-9]{3,12}$","type":"string"}},"required":["body"],"type":"object"},"name":"add_comment"},{"description":"Add labels to an existing GitHub issue or pull request for categorization and filtering. Labels must already exist in the repository. For creating new issues with labels, use create_issue with the labels property instead. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 7 label(s) can be added. Target: *.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"item_number":{"description":"Issue or PR number to add labels to. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 456 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/456). If omitted, adds labels to the item that triggered this workflow.","type":"number"},"labels":{"description":"Label names to add (e.g., ['bug', 'priority-high']). Labels must exist in the repository. This field is required — omitting it will cause a validation error.","items":{"type":"string"},"type":"array"},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"}},"required":["labels"],"type":"object"},"name":"add_labels"},{"description":"Remove labels from an existing GitHub issue or pull request. If omitted, removes labels from the item that triggered this workflow.","type":"number"},"labels":{"description":"Label names to remove (e.g., ['bug', 'needs-triage']). This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 543 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/543). 2026-04-18T18:11:38.1540776Z {"timestamp":"2026-04-18T18:10:08.700197512Z","direction":"IN","type":"RESPONSE","server_id":"github","payload":{"id":1,"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":{"content":[{"text":"{\"number\":58331,\"title\":\"[Storage] Docs: Document snapshot and version transfer support in Azure.Storage.DataMovement.Blobs\",\"body\":\"## Documentation Gap\\n\\n**Package:** `Azure.Storage.DataMovement.Blobs`\\n**Service directory:** `sdk/storage/Azure.Storage.DataMovement.Blobs/`\\n**Triggered by:** commit 44777ce3 (PR #57368) by `@amnguye`\\n\\n## What Changed\\n\\nPR #57368 added snapshot and versioning support to the `Azure.Storage.DataMovement.Blobs` package (targeting 12.4.0-beta.1). Two new properties were added to `BlobStorageResourceOptions`:\\n\\n- `public string Snapshot { get; set; }` — specifies a blob snapshot identifier to use as the transfer source\\n- `public string VersionId { get; set; }` — specifies a blob version identifier to use as the transfer source\\n\\nThe CHANGELOG correctly lists these features:\\n\\u0026gt; - Added support for snapshot transfers as the source. This includes the ability to copy a snapshot to a new blob, download a snapshot, and pause/resume snapshot transfers.\\n\\u0026gt; - Added support for versioning transfers as the source. This includes the ability to copy a version to a new blob, download a version, and pause/resume version transfers.\\n\\n## Gaps Found\\n\\n- The `README.md` has no section or examples showing how to use `BlobStorageResourceOptions.Snapshot` to transfer from a blob snapshot\\n- The `README.md` has no section or examples showing how to use `BlobStorageResourceOptions.VersionId` to transfer from a blob version\\n- No sample test file (`tests/Samples/`) contains snippet-backed examples for snapshot or version transfer scenarios\\n- The existing `ResourceConstruction_Blobs_WithOptions_BlockBlob` snippet shows `BlockBlobStorageResourceOptions` but does not demonstrate the new `Snapshot`/`VersionId` properties\\n\\n\\n\\u003cstrong\\u003e📐 Implementation Guide\\u003c/strong\\u003e\\n\\nThis section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above.\\n\\n### Step 1: Modify files\\n\\nNo existing files need structural changes. The README and sample test file need additions only.\\n\\n### Step 2: Add or update code snippets\\n\\n#### 2a. Create/update the sample test file\\n\\n**Edit** `sdk/storage/Azure.Storage.DataMovement.Blobs/tests/Samples/Sample2_Snapshot.cs` (create if it does not exist):\\n\\n````csharp\\n// Copyright (c) Microsoft Corporation. All rights reserved.\\n// Licensed under the MIT License.\\n\\nusing System.Threading.Tasks;\\nusing Azure.Core;\\nusing Azure.Storage.DataMovement;\\nusing Azure.Storage.DataMovement.Blobs;\\nusing NUnit.Framework;\\n\\nnamespace Azure.Storage.DataMovement.Blobs.Tests.Samples\\n{\\n    public class Sample2_Snapshot : SamplesBase\\n    {\\n        [Test]\\n        public async Task CopyBlobSnapshotAsync()\\n        {\\n            TokenCredential tokenCredential = TestEnvironment.Credential;\\n            BlobsStorageResourceProvider blobs = new(tokenCredential);\\n            TransferManager transferManager = new TransferManager();\\n            Uri sourceBlobUri = new Uri(\\u0026#34;(myaccount.blob.core.windows.net/redacted)\\n            Uri destinationBlobUri = new Uri(\\u0026#34;(myaccount.blob.core.windows.net/redacted)\\n            string snapshotId = \\u0026#34;\\u0026#34;;\\n            string downloadPath = System.IO.Path.GetTempFileName();\\n\\n            #region Snippet:BlobSnapshotTransfer\\n            // Copy a blob snapshot to a new blob\\n            BlockBlobStorageResourceOptions sourceSnapshotOptions = new BlockBlobStorageResourceOptions\\n            {\\n                Snapshot = snapshotId\\n            };\\n            TransferOperation copyOperation = await transferManager.StartTransferAsync(\\n                sourceResource: await blobs.FromBlobAsync(sourceBlobUri, sourceSnapshotOptions),\\n                destinationResource: await blobs.FromBlobAsync(destinationBlobUri));\\n            await copyOperation.WaitForCompletionAsync();\\n            #endregion\\n\\n            #region Snippet:BlobSnapshotDownload\\n            // Download a blob snapshot to a local file\\n            BlockBlobStorageResourceOptions snapshotOptions = new BlockBlobStorageResourceOptions\\n            {\\n                Snapshot = snapshotId\\n            };\\n            TransferOperation downloadOperation = await transferManager.StartTransferAsync(\\n                sourceResource: await blobs.FromBlobAsync(sourceBlobUri, snapshotOptions),\\n                destinationResource: LocalFilesStorageResourceProvider.FromFile(downloadPath));\\n            await downloadOperation.WaitForCompletionAsync();\\n            #endregion\\n        }\\n\\n        [Test]\\n        public async Task CopyBlobVersionAsync()\\n        {\\n            TokenCredential tokenCredential = TestEnvironment.Credential;\\n            BlobsStorageResourceProvider blobs = new(tokenCredential);\\n            TransferManager transferManager = new TransferManager();\\n            Uri sourceBlobUri = new Uri(\\u0026#34;(myaccount.blob.core.windows.net/redacted)\\n            Uri destinationBlobUri = new Uri(\\u0026#34;(myaccount.blob.core.windows.net/redacted)\\n            string versionId = \\u0026#34;\\u0026#34;;\\n            string downloadPath = System.IO.Path.GetTempFileName();\\n\\n            #region Snippet:BlobVersionTransfer\\n            // Copy a specific blob version to a new blob\\n            BlockBlobStorageResourceOptions sourceVersionOptions = new BlockBlobStorageResourceOptions\\n            {\\n                VersionId = versionId\\n            };\\n            TransferOperation copyOperation = await transferManager.StartTransferAsync(\\n                sourceResource: await blobs.FromBlobAsync(sourceBlobUri, sourceVersionOptions),\\n                destinationResource: await blobs.FromBlobAsync(destinationBlobUri));\\n            await copyOperation.WaitForCompletionAsync();\\n            #endregion\\n\\n            #region Snippet:BlobVersionDownload\\n            // Download a specific blob version to a local file\\n            BlockBlobStorageResourceOptions versionOptions = new BlockBlobStorageResourceOptions\\n            {\\n                VersionId = versionId\\n            };\\n            TransferOperation downloadOperation = await transferManager.StartTransferAsync(\\n                sourceResource: await blobs.FromBlobAsync(sourceBlobUri, versionOptions),\\n                destinationResource: LocalFilesStorageResourceProvider.FromFile(downloadPath));\\n            await downloadOperation.WaitForCompletionAsync();\\n            #endregion\\n        }\\n    }\\n}\\n````\\n\\nSnippet names to use:\\n- `BlobSnapshotTransfer` — copying from a snapshot\\n- `BlobSnapshotDownload` — downloading a snapshot\\n- `BlobVersionTransfer` — copying from a version\\n- `BlobVersionDownload` — downloading a version\\n\\n#### 2b. Add snippet placeholders to the README\\n\\nIn `sdk/storage/Azure.Storage.DataMovement.Blobs/README.md`, insert a new sub-section after the existing **Blob Copy** section and before the **Resume** section:\\n\\n````markdown\\n### Blob Snapshot and Version Transfers\\n\\nYou can use a blob snapshot or a specific blob version as the source of a transfer by setting `Snapshot` or `VersionId` on the `BlobStorageResourceOptions` (or any derived options class).\\n\\nCopy a blob snapshot to a new blob:\\n\\n```C# Snippet:BlobSnapshotTransfer\\n```\\n\\nDownload a blob snapshot to a local file:\\n\\n```C# Snippet:BlobSnapshotDownload\\n```\\n\\nCopy a specific blob version to a new blob:\\n\\n```C# Snippet:BlobVersionTransfer\\n```\\n\\nDownload a specific blob version to a local file:\\n\\n```C# Snippet:BlobVersionDownload\\n```\\n\\n\\u0026gt; **Note:** `Snapshot` and `VersionId` are source-only options. Setting them on the destination resource options has no effect.\\n````\\n\\n### Step 3: Verify README structure\\n\\nThe current README at `sdk/storage/Azure.Storage.DataMovement.Blobs/README.md` already has all required sections:\\n1. ✅ Getting started (Install, Prerequisites, Authenticate)\\n2. ✅ Key concepts\\n3. ✅ Examples — **needs new sub-section for snapshot/version transfers**\\n4. ✅ Troubleshooting\\n5. ✅ Next steps\\n6. ✅ Contributing\\n\\nOnly the **Examples** section needs the new sub-section added.\\n\\n### Step 4: Validate\\n\\nRun these commands in order:\\n\\n1. `dotnet build sdk/storage/Azure.Storage.DataMovement.Blobs/`\\n2. `dotnet test sdk/storage/Azure.Storage.DataMovement.Blobs/ --filter TestCategory!=Live`\\n3. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 storage` — injects snippet code from the test file into README placeholder blocks; verify `git diff` shows the README updated with actual code\\n4. `eng/scripts/Export-API.ps1 storage` — no new public types were added in this change, but run to confirm no drift\\n5. `dotnet format sdk/storage/Azure.Storage.DataMovement.Blobs/`\\n\\n\\n\\n## Next Steps\\n\\n\\u0026gt; [!TIP]\\n\\u0026gt; **Ready for automated implementation?** Assign this issue to **`@copilot`** to have Copilot coding agent implement the changes described in the Implementation Guide above\\n\\n\\n\\n\\n\\u0026gt; [!WARNING]\\n\\u0026gt; \\n\\u0026gt; \\u003cstrong\\u003e⚠️ Firewall blocked 2 domains\\u003c/strong\\u003e\\n\\u0026gt;\\n\\u0026gt; The following domains were blocked by the firewall during workflow execution:\\n\\u0026gt;\\n\\u0026gt; - `pkgs.dev.azure.com`\\n\\u0026gt; - `releaseassets.githubusercontent.com`\\n\\u0026gt;\\n\\u0026gt; To allow these domains, add them to the `network.allowed` list in your workflow frontmatter:\\n\\u0026gt;\\n\\u0026gt; ```yaml\\n\\u0026gt; network:\\n\\u0026gt;   allowed:\\n\\u0026gt;     - defaults\\n\\u0026gt;     - \\u0026#34;pkgs.dev.azure.com\\u0026#34;\\n\\u0026gt;     - \\u0026#34;releaseassets.githubusercontent.com\\u0026#34;\\n\\u0026gt; 2026-04-18T18:11:38.1610377Z {"timestamp":"2026-04-18T18:10:08.88489757Z","direction":"IN","type":"RESPONSE","server_id":"github","payload":{"id":1,"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":{"content":[{"text":"{\"number\":58331,\"title\":\"[Storage] Docs: Document snapshot and version transfer support in Azure.Storage.DataMovement.Blobs\",\"body\":\"## Documentation Gap\\n\\n**Package:** `Azure.Storage.DataMovement.Blobs`\\n**Service directory:** `sdk/storage/Azure.Storage.DataMovement.Blobs/`\\n**Triggered by:** commit 44777ce3 (PR #57368) by `@amnguye`\\n\\n## What Changed\\n\\nPR #57368 added snapshot and versioning support to the `Azure.Storage.DataMovement.Blobs` package (targeting 12.4.0-beta.1). Two new properties were added to `BlobStorageResourceOptions`:\\n\\n- `public string Snapshot { get; set; }` — specifies a blob snapshot identifier to use as the transfer source\\n- `public string VersionId { get; set; }` — specifies a blob version identifier to use as the transfer source\\n\\nThe CHANGELOG correctly lists these features:\\n\\u0026gt; - Added support for snapshot transfers as the source. This includes the ability to copy a snapshot to a new blob, download a snapshot, and pause/resume snapshot transfers.\\n\\u0026gt; - Added support for versioning transfers as the source. This includes the ability to copy a version to a new blob, download a version, and pause/resume version transfers.\\n\\n## Gaps Found\\n\\n- The `README.md` has no section or examples showing how to use `BlobStorageResourceOptions.Snapshot` to transfer from a blob snapshot\\n- The `README.md` has no section or examples showing how to use `BlobStorageResourceOptions.VersionId` to transfer from a blob version\\n- No sample test file (`tests/Samples/`) contains snippet-backed examples for snapshot or version transfer scenarios\\n- The existing `ResourceConstruction_Blobs_WithOptions_BlockBlob` snippet shows `BlockBlobStorageResourceOptions` but does not demonstrate the new `Snapshot`/`VersionId` properties\\n\\n\\n\\u003cstrong\\u003e📐 Implementation Guide\\u003c/strong\\u003e\\n\\nThis section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above.\\n\\n### Step 1: Modify files\\n\\nNo existing files need structural changes. The README and sample test file need additions only.\\n\\n### Step 2: Add or update code snippets\\n\\n#### 2a. Create/update the sample test file\\n\\n**Edit** `sdk/storage/Azure.Storage.DataMovement.Blobs/tests/Samples/Sample2_Snapshot.cs` (create if it does not exist):\\n\\n````csharp\\n// Copyright (c) Microsoft Corporation. All rights reserved.\\n// Licensed under the MIT License.\\n\\nusing System.Threading.Tasks;\\nusing Azure.Core;\\nusing Azure.Storage.DataMovement;\\nusing Azure.Storage.DataMovement.Blobs;\\nusing NUnit.Framework;\\n\\nnamespace Azure.Storage.DataMovement.Blobs.Tests.Samples\\n{\\n    public class Sample2_Snapshot : SamplesBase\\n    {\\n        [Test]\\n        public async Task CopyBlobSnapshotAsync()\\n        {\\n            TokenCredential tokenCredential = TestEnvironment.Credential;\\n            BlobsStorageResourceProvider blobs = new(tokenCredential);\\n            TransferManager transferManager = new TransferManager();\\n            Uri sourceBlobUri = new Uri(\\u0026#34;(myaccount.blob.core.windows.net/redacted)\\n            Uri destinationBlobUri = new Uri(\\u0026#34;(myaccount.blob.core.windows.net/redacted)\\n            string snapshotId = \\u0026#34;\\u0026#34;;\\n            string downloadPath = System.IO.Path.GetTempFileName();\\n\\n            #region Snippet:BlobSnapshotTransfer\\n            // Copy a blob snapshot to a new blob\\n            BlockBlobStorageResourceOptions sourceSnapshotOptions = new BlockBlobStorageResourceOptions\\n            {\\n                Snapshot = snapshotId\\n            };\\n            TransferOperation copyOperation = await transferManager.StartTransferAsync(\\n                sourceResource: await blobs.FromBlobAsync(sourceBlobUri, sourceSnapshotOptions),\\n                destinationResource: await blobs.FromBlobAsync(destinationBlobUri));\\n            await copyOperation.WaitForCompletionAsync();\\n            #endregion\\n\\n            #region Snippet:BlobSnapshotDownload\\n            // Download a blob snapshot to a local file\\n            BlockBlobStorageResourceOptions snapshotOptions = new BlockBlobStorageResourceOptions\\n            {\\n                Snapshot = snapshotId\\n            };\\n            TransferOperation downloadOperation = await transferManager.StartTransferAsync(\\n                sourceResource: await blobs.FromBlobAsync(sourceBlobUri, snapshotOptions),\\n                destinationResource: LocalFilesStorageResourceProvider.FromFile(downloadPath));\\n            await downloadOperation.WaitForCompletionAsync();\\n            #endregion\\n        }\\n\\n        [Test]\\n        public async Task CopyBlobVersionAsync()\\n        {\\n            TokenCredential tokenCredential = TestEnvironment.Credential;\\n            BlobsStorageResourceProvider blobs = new(tokenCredential);\\n            TransferManager transferManager = new TransferManager();\\n            Uri sourceBlobUri = new Uri(\\u0026#34;(myaccount.blob.core.windows.net/redacted)\\n            Uri destinationBlobUri = new Uri(\\u0026#34;(myaccount.blob.core.windows.net/redacted)\\n            string versionId = \\u0026#34;\\u0026#34;;\\n            string downloadPath = System.IO.Path.GetTempFileName();\\n\\n            #region Snippet:BlobVersionTransfer\\n            // Copy a specific blob version to a new blob\\n            BlockBlobStorageResourceOptions sourceVersionOptions = new BlockBlobStorageResourceOptions\\n            {\\n                VersionId = versionId\\n            };\\n            TransferOperation copyOperation = await transferManager.StartTransferAsync(\\n                sourceResource: await blobs.FromBlobAsync(sourceBlobUri, sourceVersionOptions),\\n                destinationResource: await blobs.FromBlobAsync(destinationBlobUri));\\n            await copyOperation.WaitForCompletionAsync();\\n            #endregion\\n\\n            #region Snippet:BlobVersionDownload\\n            // Download a specific blob version to a local file\\n            BlockBlobStorageResourceOptions versionOptions = new BlockBlobStorageResourceOptions\\n            {\\n                VersionId = versionId\\n            };\\n            TransferOperation downloadOperation = await transferManager.StartTransferAsync(\\n                sourceResource: await blobs.FromBlobAsync(sourceBlobUri, versionOptions),\\n                destinationResource: LocalFilesStorageResourceProvider.FromFile(downloadPath));\\n            await downloadOperation.WaitForCompletionAsync();\\n            #endregion\\n        }\\n    }\\n}\\n````\\n\\nSnippet names to use:\\n- `BlobSnapshotTransfer` — copying from a snapshot\\n- `BlobSnapshotDownload` — downloading a snapshot\\n- `BlobVersionTransfer` — copying from a version\\n- `BlobVersionDownload` — downloading a version\\n\\n#### 2b. Add snippet placeholders to the README\\n\\nIn `sdk/storage/Azure.Storage.DataMovement.Blobs/README.md`, insert a new sub-section after the existing **Blob Copy** section and before the **Resume** section:\\n\\n````markdown\\n### Blob Snapshot and Version Transfers\\n\\nYou can use a blob snapshot or a specific blob version as the source of a transfer by setting `Snapshot` or `VersionId` on the `BlobStorageResourceOptions` (or any derived options class).\\n\\nCopy a blob snapshot to a new blob:\\n\\n```C# Snippet:BlobSnapshotTransfer\\n```\\n\\nDownload a blob snapshot to a local file:\\n\\n```C# Snippet:BlobSnapshotDownload\\n```\\n\\nCopy a specific blob version to a new blob:\\n\\n```C# Snippet:BlobVersionTransfer\\n```\\n\\nDownload a specific blob version to a local file:\\n\\n```C# Snippet:BlobVersionDownload\\n```\\n\\n\\u0026gt; **Note:** `Snapshot` and `VersionId` are source-only options. Setting them on the destination resource options has no effect.\\n````\\n\\n### Step 3: Verify README structure\\n\\nThe current README at `sdk/storage/Azure.Storage.DataMovement.Blobs/README.md` already has all required sections:\\n1. ✅ Getting started (Install, Prerequisites, Authenticate)\\n2. ✅ Key concepts\\n3. ✅ Examples — **needs new sub-section for snapshot/version transfers**\\n4. ✅ Troubleshooting\\n5. ✅ Next steps\\n6. ✅ Contributing\\n\\nOnly the **Examples** section needs the new sub-section added.\\n\\n### Step 4: Validate\\n\\nRun these commands in order:\\n\\n1. `dotnet build sdk/storage/Azure.Storage.DataMovement.Blobs/`\\n2. `dotnet test sdk/storage/Azure.Storage.DataMovement.Blobs/ --filter TestCategory!=Live`\\n3. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 storage` — injects snippet code from the test file into README placeholder blocks; verify `git diff` shows the README updated with actual code\\n4. `eng/scripts/Export-API.ps1 storage` — no new public types were added in this change, but run to confirm no drift\\n5. `dotnet format sdk/storage/Azure.Storage.DataMovement.Blobs/`\\n\\n\\n\\n## Next Steps\\n\\n\\u0026gt;
